name: Valle del Lozoya – circular to Buitrago from the Embalse de Pinilla – gravel
name_original: Valle del Lozoya – circular hasta Buitrago desde el Embalse de Pinilla – gravel
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Lozoya are set within the Sierra Norte of Madrid, characterized by the picturesque Lozoya Valley, which is flanked by the Los Montes mountain chain and La Cuerda Larga mountain range. The region features diverse ecosystems, including riverside forests, pine groves, oak groves, ash groves, and holm oaks, consistently accompanied by the Lozoya River. Terrain varies from mostly paved surfaces to unpaved and challenging gravel segments, with elevations exceeding two thousand meters in the Sierra de Guadarrama. Best touring cycling routes around Lozoya The most popular touring cycling route is Valle del Lozoya – circular to Buitrago from the Embalse de Pinilla – gravel, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a gravel experience with views of the Embalse de Pinilla. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monastery of El Paular – Las Presillas del Lozoya loop from Coto de pesca, a moderate 22.9 miles (36.9 km) path. This loop passes by the historic Monastery of El Paular, set amidst pine and oak forests. Local touring cyclists also love the Morcuera–Canencia Track – Canto Bridge of Canencia loop from Lozoya, a 26.7 miles (43.0 km) trail leading through the Morcuera–Canencia Track, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Lozoya is defined by the Lozoya Valley, the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, and the Canencia Pass, featuring diverse landscapes from dense forests to mountain passes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Lozoya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lozoya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Lozoya?
A: Lozoya offers a diverse selection of over 18 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels and preferences. These routes explore the picturesque Lozoya Valley and the surrounding Sierra de Guadarrama mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Lozoya?
A: The terrain in Lozoya is quite varied. You'll find mostly paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels, but also unpaved and challenging gravel segments. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961417906/valle-del-lozoya-circular-to-buitrago-from-the-embalse-de-pinilla-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Lozoya – circular to Buitrago from the Embalse de Pinilla – gravel&lt;/a&gt; specifically offer a gravel experience, while others may combine paved and unpaved sections, especially around areas like the Canencia Pass.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Lozoya?
A: While many routes in Lozoya are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and less technical terrain, making them ideal for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride through the scenic landscapes.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in Lozoya?
A: Lozoya's routes are rich with points of interest. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of El Paular&lt;/a&gt;, explore the well-preserved village of Buitrago del Lozoya, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojonavalle Forest&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse scenery from pine groves to riverside forests.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Lozoya?
A: Yes, Lozoya offers several excellent circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14886224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of El Paular – Las Presillas del Lozoya loop from Coto de pesca&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 22.9-mile path that takes you past the historic monastery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40348902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morcuera–Canencia Track – Canto Bridge of Canencia loop from Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;, a more challenging 26.7-mile trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Lozoya?
A: Lozoya is an appealing destination for touring cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. While summer can be warm, the higher elevations provide cooler air. Winter cycling is possible, but routes may be affected by snow or colder conditions, especially in the higher parts of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Can I find family-friendly touring cycling options in Lozoya?
A: For families, it's best to look for the easier routes, which typically feature less elevation gain and smoother surfaces. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always present, the region's diverse trails mean that with careful selection, you can find paths suitable for a leisurely family ride, especially those along the Lozoya River or around the reservoirs.

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes in Lozoya?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as Rascafría, Lozoya, or near the Embalse de Pinilla. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas, especially during peak seasons.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Lozoya?
A: The touring cycling routes in Lozoya are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning natural beauty of the Lozoya Valley, the diverse terrain, and the well-maintained paths that offer a rewarding experience for various skill levels.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Lozoya?
A: Absolutely. Lozoya offers 13 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders seeking a challenge. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19831013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Navafría – Navafría Pass loop from Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.2-mile route with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience through mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that feature natural pools or lakes for a refreshing stop?
A: Yes, several routes in Lozoya offer views or access to natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14886224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of El Paular – Las Presillas del Lozoya loop from Coto de pesca&lt;/a&gt; passes by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5945445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Presillas del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;, which includes natural pools perfect for a refreshing break during your ride.

Q: Can I access Lozoya's cycling routes using public transport?
A: While direct public transport access to every trailhead might be limited, several towns in the Lozoya Valley, such as Rascafría and Buitrago del Lozoya, are accessible by bus from Madrid. From these points, you can often connect to various cycling routes, making it possible to reach the region without a car.
tips: It is a unique town that preserves its complete wall, the only one in the region and declared a National Monument in 1931. Don't miss its Gothic-Mudejar style Alcázar with a clear Arab influence. A storybook and postcard town like few others.
Beautiful stone bridge over the Lozoya River, very characteristic of this area. The canyon of the river provides a very picturesque environment that will give you some unique postcards.

It is a privileged place, surrounded by majestic peaks of the Sierra de Guadarrama and nestled in the upper course of the Lozoya River.

In 1964 the people of Madrid had to submit to numerous restrictions and this was precisely the year in which the Pinilla dam was built, very close to Pinilla del Valle.

La Pinilla is one of the five reservoirs of the Lozoya River, along with El Atazar, Riosequillo, El Villar and Puentes Viejas.
